summary: The European Union coordinates the post-quantum transition through Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 and a Coordinated Implementation Roadmap, asking Member States to migrate in a synchronised way, with the Quantum Europe Strategy confirming that the EU and Member States are implementing the Recommendation. ENISA provides technical studies, and through the EUCC certification scheme ENISA and the ECCG have agreed several asymmetric post-quantum mechanisms and hybrid key-combiner mechanisms for products seeking certification. ETSI develops European quantum-safe standards including a hybrid key exchange standard. A 2026 Commission proposal would require Member States to adopt post-quantum migration policies.
processParticipation:
ENISA post-quantum standardisation studies | Surveys and reports on the state of PQC standardisation
ETSI TC ESI PQC Working Group | Develops post-quantum signatures, certificates, public key infrastructures and transition policies
EUCC Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms (ENISA / ECCG) | Agrees post-quantum and hybrid cryptographic mechanisms for ICT products under EUCC certification
govActors:
European Commission | Issues the Coordinated Implementation Roadmap and Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101, the Quantum Europe Strategy, and the proposal to require Member State PQC migration policies
ENISA | EU Agency for Cybersecurity, publishes studies on the state of post-quantum standardisation and integration and co-publishes the EUCC Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms
ECCG | European Cybersecurity Certification Group, agrees the EUCC cryptographic mechanisms including post-quantum and hybrid key-combiner mechanisms
ETSI | European standards body developing quantum-safe standards including a hybrid key exchange standard and the TC ESI PQC working group
NIS Cooperation Group | Sets out the Coordinated Implementation Roadmap recommendations for Member States
European Parliament | Resolution on the EU Cybersecurity Strategy calling for further research into post-quantum algorithms
EPRS | European Parliamentary Research Service, briefings on cryptographic security, digital sovereignty and quantum technologies identifying PQC as the near-to-medium-term priority
EESC | Opinion noting the need to transition to quantum-resistant cryptography
ECB | Speeches and the Pontes user requirements document requiring post-quantum resilient cryptographic capabilities through crypto-agility
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 | EU | binding-law
EUCC certification scheme, Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms v2 | EU | binding-by-market-access
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on PQC | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2025) | EU | soft-law
Proposal COM(2026) 13 final, mandatory Member State PQC migration policies | EU | soft-law
Quantum Europe Strategy, COM(2025) 363 final | EU | soft-law
